evidence: MultiSeedExplorer.explore is cleanly decomposed into 4 named phase helpers (fetchCollocatesPerSeed, identifyCommonCollocates, buildDiscoveredNouns, buildCoreCollocates) each with a focused responsibility and clear name
SemanticFieldExplorer is a facade delegating to MultiSeedExplorer and CollocateProfileComparator — the single-responsibility split is explicit and documented in the class Javadoc

ExplorationHandlers.validateExploreRequest bundles the three-step validation preamble (parse, require seeds, resolve relation, resolve numeric params) into a single ValidatedExploreRequest record — reduces the per-handler setup boilerplate from ~10 lines to 3

SketchHandlers.executeRelationQueries extracts the shared iteration/execution loop shared by handleFullSketchForType and handleDepSketchForType, eliminating duplication via a BiFunction builder callback — good structural refactoring

compareCollocateProfiles is not covered by ExploreOptions — the single exploration class has two different calling conventions (ExploreOptions for explore/multi, raw params for comparison), requiring callers to remember which method takes which form

ExplorationHandlers.resolveExploreParams extracts nounsPerCollocate (only applicable to single-seed) alongside the shared params (top, minShared, minLogDice, nounsPerCollocate), then the multi-seed handler explicitly rejects 'nouns_per' if provided — the shared params object bundles a param that is not shared

WordSketchApiServer.registerRoutes contains 11 registerGet/registerPost calls — the route table is data that could be expressed more compactly but is not complex enough to warrant a data-driven route registry; acceptable at current scale

CollocateQueryHelper.collectHits and scoreHits represent a two-phase pipeline within the BCQL query path; the pipeline is clearly named and the record type HitRecord carries exactly what phase 2 needs from phase 1 — good design

SemanticFieldExplorer.deriveNounCqlConstraint has nested stream+orElseGet logic (find OBJECT_OF/SUBJECT_OF → fallback to any NOUN collocate → fallback to constant) — the nesting is justified by the 3-level priority chain but is harder to read than the named-phase pattern used elsewhere

ExploreResponseBuilder (static utility) holds all response-building logic for three different caller paths (explore, multi-seed, comparison) — the consolidation is correct but the class is growing; if a fourth response type is added it may need splitting

strengths: Phase decomposition pattern is consistently applied in the most complex algorithms: SemanticFieldExplorer's 4-phase exploration and MultiSeedExplorer's 4-phase multi-seed both use named private helpers with clear, bounded responsibilities
Facade delegation pattern in SemanticFieldExplorer is clean and explicitly documented — the class does not mix algorithmic concerns with the objects it delegates to
Value objects (ExploreOptions, ValidatedExploreRequest, ExploreParams) reduce parameter list length and prevent positional argument confusion at the three most call-heavy code paths
SketchHandlers.executeRelationQueries successfully eliminates duplicated relation-iteration loops that existed before the BiFunction callback refactoring
design_coherence.issue_character: Design issues are not systemic but rather two localized cross-cutting concerns: ExploreOptions bundles a single-seed-only parameter (nounsPerCollocate) alongside shared params, and compareCollocateProfiles was not included in the ExploreOptions migration, creating a split calling convention within one class.
design_coherence.score_rationale: The codebase demonstrates solid structural thinking: phase decomposition, facade delegation, and value-object parameter bundling are all applied consistently where they matter most. The identified design gaps are real but localized: the ExploreOptions/raw-params split in SemanticFieldExplorer and the nounsPerCollocate leakage into the shared options object. These affect a moderately important code path but do not represent a systemic design failure. Against a 80-anchor for 'solid but uneven', the codebase sits just below at 79 — the good design decisions outweigh the rough edges by a meaningful margin.

issues.summary: ExploreOptions.nounsPerCollocate is a single-seed-only parameter bundled into the shared options record, causing the multi-seed handler to explicitly reject it
evidence: ExploreOptions record contains nounsPerCollocate annotated 'Applies to single-seed exploration only; pass 0 (via forMultiSeed) for multi-seed'

ExplorationHandlers.handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti: `if (req.params().containsKey("nouns_per")) { sendError(..., "nouns_per is not supported for multi-seed"); return; }` — rejecting a param that the shared validation already parsed
resolveExploreParams always parses nounsPerCollocate even for the multi-seed path where it will be discarded and replaced by ExploreOptions.forMultiSeed()
issues.suggestion: Split ExploreOptions into SharedExploreOptions (top, minLogDice, minShared) and SingleSeedExploreOptions extends SharedExploreOptions (adds nounsPerCollocate); or keep ExploreOptions as-is but stop validating nouns_per in the shared preamble for multi-seed — validate it only in the single-seed handler

issues.summary: compareCollocateProfiles accepts raw (Set, double, int) parameters while sibling methods on SemanticFieldExplorer use ExploreOptions, creating a split calling convention within the same class
evidence: exploreByPattern(String, RelationConfig, ExploreOptions) and exploreMultiSeed(Set, RelationConfig, ExploreOptions) both use ExploreOptions
compareCollocateProfiles(Set<String> seedNouns, double minLogDice, int maxPerNoun) uses positional raw params

ExplorationHandlers.handleSemanticFieldComparison must manually unpack exploreParams.topCollocates() and exploreParams.minLogDice() to pass to compareCollocateProfiles, while the explore handlers pass ExploreOptions directly
issues.suggestion: Add a compareCollocateProfiles(Set<String>, ExploreOptions) overload that delegates to the existing method; update handleSemanticFieldComparison to pass ExploreOptions for design coherence across the SemanticFieldExplorer public API

root_causes: Incremental ExploreOptions migration: the value-object refactoring was applied to explore paths but not backfilled to compareCollocateProfiles; nounsPerCollocate was included in ExploreOptions because it was added at the same time rather than being evaluated for scope
